dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the barricades held. Every family is accounted for.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {quest_item} you gathered reinforced the walls just in time. The swarm hit at dawn and the defenses held every point. {target_npc} led the counterattack from the eastern gate and drove them back into the hills. The danger is over, at least for now. I stood in the square this morning and told everyone the truth: that my recklessness caused the swarm. Some people were angry. Most were just relieved to be alive.
dialogueChunks.outro: You cleaned up my mess and saved lives doing it. I owe you more than I can ever repay. But start with this.

playerResponses.text: Telling the truth took courage. The settlement will respect that.
The swarm is driven back and the walls held. That's what matters.

dialogueChunks.intro: The soil is recovering, {player_name}. The alchemist says the readings are back to normal.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The mineral you found in that cave worked exactly as they predicted. Within two days of application, the contamination stopped spreading. Within a week, the first green shoots pushed through. {target_npc} planted a test crop yesterday and it's already taking root. I confessed to the settlement about the experimental compound. The alchemist spoke on my behalf: said the lesson I learned will make me a better apothecary, not a worse one.
dialogueChunks.outro: The harvest won't be what it should have been, but it will be enough. Take this, {player_name}. You saved a season and possibly a career.

playerResponses.text: Green shoots from poisoned ground. That's as close to a miracle as it gets.
Learn from this. Test before you deploy. Always.
Glad the land is healing. Take better care of it.

dialogueChunks.intro: You found it, {player_name}. The source of the signal.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The device you recovered from the chamber was ancient: some kind of resonance beacon that attracted and agitated the {enemy_type} within range. Once you disabled it, the coordinated attacks stopped within hours. {target_npc} reports the creatures are already dispersing back to their normal territories. The alchemist analyzed the {quest_item} coating and says the beacon was likely placed there deliberately, centuries ago, as a weapon. I just happened to set it off.
dialogueChunks.outro: An ancient weapon armed by modern carelessness. At least it's over now. Take this, and my genuine thanks.

playerResponses.text: Ancient traps should stay sealed. Remember that next time you find a mysterious door.
The beacon is destroyed and the creatures are scattering. Good enough.
Centuries-old weapons lurking underground. The world is full of dangers nobody remembers.

dialogueChunks.intro: You found the con artist, {player_name}. And you brought back our supplies.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The swindler was hiding in a cave {location_hint}, surrounded by the very {enemy_type} they'd been trading with. When you showed up, they realized the game was over. {target_npc} has already distributed the recovered {quest_item} to the families who needed it most. I stood in front of the council and told them everything: the gamble, the scam, my stupidity. They stripped me of my trading privileges for a season. Fair punishment.
dialogueChunks.outro: You recovered what my greed nearly lost forever. The settlement is whole again, and I have a lot of trust to rebuild. This is yours, earned honestly.

playerResponses.text: A season's punishment is light for what happened. Use the time to reflect.
The supplies are back where they belong. That's what counts.
Next time a deal sounds too good to be true, it probably is.
